title: Incomplete storage.conf results in artifacts created in system root dir
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am working in Debian unstable, with podman 3.4.7 installed, and have noticed the following items pop up in the root directory:
```
# ls -l /
total 88
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root     7 Apr 25 21:09 bin -> usr/bin
drwxr-xr-x   3 root root  4096 Apr 25 21:50 boot
drwxr-xr-x  17 root root  3180 Apr 25 22:25 dev
drwxr-xr-x  61 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:25 etc
drwxr-xr-x   6 root root  4096 Apr 25 21:00 home
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root    30 Apr 25 21:11 initrd.img -> boot/initrd.img-5.17.0-1-amd64
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root    30 Apr 25 21:11 initrd.img.old -> boot/initrd.img-5.17.0-1-amd64
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root     7 Apr 25 21:09 lib -> usr/lib
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root     9 Apr 25 21:09 lib32 -> usr/lib32
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root     9 Apr 25 21:09 lib64 -> usr/lib64
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root    10 Apr 25 21:09 libx32 -> usr/libx32
drwx------   2 root root 16384 Apr 25 21:39 lost+found
drwxr-xr-x   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 21:09 media
drwxr-xr-x   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 21:09 mnt
drwx------   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:25 mounts                <---(!)
drwxr-xr-x   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 21:09 opt
drwx------   3 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:25 overlay               <---(!)
drwx------   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:25 overlay-containers    <---(!)
drwx------   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:25 overlay-images        <---(!)
drwx------   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:25 overlay-layers        <---(!)
drwx------   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:25 overlay-locks         <---(!)
dr-xr-xr-x 128 root root     0 Apr 25 22:24 proc
drwx------  10 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:28 root
drwxr-xr-x  19 root root   560 Apr 25 22:26 run
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root     8 Apr 25 21:09 sbin -> usr/sbin
drwxr-xr-x   2 root root  4096 Apr 25 21:09 srv
-rw-r--r--   1 root root    64 Apr 25 22:25 storage.lock          <---(!)
dr-xr-xr-x  13 root root     0 Apr 25 22:24 sys
drwxrwxrwt   7 root root  4096 Apr 25 22:28 tmp
-rw-r--r--   1 root root     0 Apr 25 22:25 userns.lock           <---(!)
drwxr-xr-x  14 root root  4096 Apr 25 21:09 usr
drwxr-xr-x  11 root root  4096 Apr 25 21:09 var
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root    27 Apr 25 21:11 vmlinuz -> boot/vmlinuz-5.17.0-1-amd64
lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root    27 Apr 25 21:11 vmlinuz.old -> boot/vmlinuz-5.17.0-1-amd64
```
These directories/files are created on startup. Needless to say, they do not belong there.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman normally.

2. Add the following `/etc/containers/storage.conf` file:
   ```
   [storage]
   driver = "overlay"
   ```

3. `rm -rf /var/lib/containers/storage`

4. Reboot, and check the root directory.

**Describe the results you received:**

The above-listed directories/files are present in the root directory.

**Describe the results you expected:**

These directories/files should continue to be created in the default location (`/var/lib/containers/storage/`).

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Diagnosing this issue was complicated by the fact that [some of] podman's configuration is cached in a database file. I had great difficulty reproducing the behavior reliably before figuring out that step 3 above was necessary.

Here are podman's messages to `/var/log/syslog` on system boot, with `--log-level=debug` edited into the systemd unit files. Notice how it repeatedly says it will use the default graph root of `/var/lib/containers/storage`, but then you see `"Using graph root "` and then `"Using graph root ."` (likewise for "run root"):
```
Apr 25 22:43:55 image-debian64 podman[457]: time="2022-04-25T22:43:55-04:00" level=warning msg="Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root \"/var/lib/containers/storage\""
Apr 25 22:43:55 image-debian64 podman[513]: time="2022-04-25T22:43:55-04:00" level=warning msg="Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root \"/var/lib/containers/storage\""
Apr 25 22:43:55 image-debian64 podman[458]: time="2022-04-25T22:43:55-04:00" level=warning msg="Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root \"/var/lib/containers/storage\""
Apr 25 22:43:56 image-debian64 podman[513]: time="2022-04-25T22:43:56-04:00" level=warning msg="Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root \"/var/lib/containers/storage\""

```

```
Go Version:   go1.18.1
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
  memFree: 3866062848
  memTotal: 4120723456

    name: crun
    package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'

    version: |-

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: true
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.0.1

      libslirp: 4.6.1
  swapFree: 541061120

  uptime: 14m 8.84s

  log:
  - k8s-file
  - none
  - journald
  network:
  - bridge
  - macvlan
  volume:
  - local
registries: {}
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /root
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"

  runRoot: /root
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
# apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unstable,now 3.4.7+ds1-3 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

I have attempted to reproduce this issue with podman 4.0.3 from Debian experimental, but it does not appear to occur there.

I looked through the Troubleshooting Guide. It does not appear to cover this situation.

[This old issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7419) appears to be related, but was closed due to an unrelated resolution.

comments.body:
I don't think we plan another 3.4 release. if so it is only for security issues.
So if it works with 4.0.3 I think we can close this.
